title: Annotating Kubernetes Resources
description:
## Annotating Kubernetes Resources

Annotations attach arbitrary non-identifying metadata to Kubernetes objects — such as contact info, tool versions, or documentation URLs. Unlike labels, annotations **cannot** be used as selectors.

**Your task:**

A Deployment named `myapp` is running in the `default` namespace. Add the following annotations to it:
- `contact=ops@company.com`
- `reviewed=true`

```bash
# Verify:
kubectl get deployment myapp -o jsonpath='{.metadata.annotations}'
```

hints.title: kubectl annotate
hints.body: Use `kubectl annotate` to imperatively add or overwrite annotations on any resource.
hints.command: kubectl annotate deployment myapp contact=ops@company.com reviewed=true

hints.title: Overwrite an existing annotation
hints.body: If the annotation key already exists, pass `--overwrite` to update it.
hints.command: kubectl annotate deployment myapp contact=ops@company.com reviewed=true --overwrite
